types.name: PRECISION_TRUNCATION
types.description: Narrowing integer or pointer-width cast that loses bits (size_t to int, uint64_t to uint32_t, long to int). Common in parsers and length arithmetic.

types.name: MULT_BEFORE_DIV
types.description: Expression of the form a * b / c where the intermediate product can overflow a fixed-width integer type before the division runs.

types.name: CHECKED_ARITHMETIC_GAP
types.description: Mix of __builtin_*_overflow or SafeInt<> wrappers with raw +/-/* on one code path. A single unchecked operation anchors integer-based memory-corruption chains.

types.name: ZERO_AMOUNT_PASSTHROUGH
types.description: Zero-length passthrough: malloc(0) treated as valid, memcpy with n=0 and invalid src/dst pointer (UB on some platforms), zero-length read into unvalidated buffer.

types.name: LOOP_CONTAINER_MUTATION
types.description: Iteration over an STL container (or linked list) while the same body mutates it. Iterator invalidation or skipped/duplicated elements follow.
markers: for (auto it = v.begin(); it != v.end(); ++it) { v.push_back

types.name: UNBOUNDED_ITERATION
types.description: Loop bound derived from attacker-shaped data with no cap: while(!queue.empty()), linked-list traversal, recursive descent on untrusted input.

types.name: COUNTER_INCREMENT
types.description: Monotonic counter (next_id, seq, atomic_fetch_add). Off-by-one, unsigned wrap, and non-atomic increment on a shared counter are all hazards.

types.name: CROSS_MODULE_CALL
types.description: Dynamic cross-module entry: dlopen/dlsym lookup, function-pointer call resolved at runtime, plugin entry point, IPC call. Anchors reentrancy and callback-side-effect chains.

types.name: INVARIANT_PRESERVATION
types.description: Declared invariant (assert, static_assert, BOOST_ASSERT) over multiple state variables. The composed state may drift from the asserted relation on some path.

types.name: USE_AFTER_FREE
types.description: Read or write of memory after its lifetime ended (free(p); ... *p; or delete obj; obj->m();).

types.name: DOUBLE_FREE
types.description: Two calls to free/delete on the same pointer, often across an error path and a cleanup path.
markers: goto err1; ... goto err2; both free(buf)

types.name: HEAP_BUFFER_OVERFLOW
types.description: Write or read past the end of a heap allocation. Composed with A06 (unchecked arithmetic) or M02/M05 (unchecked length from external input).

types.name: STACK_BUFFER_OVERFLOW
types.description: Write past the end of a stack buffer, including via variable-length arrays and alloca.

types.name: MISSING_NUL_TERMINATOR
types.description: String treated as NUL-terminated when it is not: strncpy without explicit terminator, read() buffer handed to strlen.

types.name: ZERO_SIZE_ALLOC
types.description: malloc(0) / calloc(0, n) / new T[0] whose return value is then treated as a valid, non-null, writable allocation. Implementation-defined behavior.
markers: count * sizeof(T) where count may be 0

types.name: OOB_READ
types.description: Read past the end of an allocation or array. Distinct from J03 because reads are often information leaks rather than corruption.

types.name: NULL_DEREF_AFTER_CHECK
types.description: Pointer checked against NULL then dereferenced on a path where the check does not in fact guarantee non-NULL (different branch, different lock scope, intervening reset).

types.name: DANGLING_POINTER
types.description: Pointer or reference whose pointee has been destroyed (stack frame returned, container resized, RAII object went out of scope).

types.name: ITERATOR_INVALIDATION
types.description: Iterator or reference into an STL container used after a mutation that the standard marks as invalidating (push_back, insert, erase, reserve, resize).

types.name: RETURN_ADDRESS_OF_LOCAL
types.description: Function returns a pointer or reference to a local (stack) object. Caller reads freed stack.

types.name: SHARED_PTR_CYCLE
types.description: std::shared_ptr reference cycle keeps objects live forever; pure memory leak with no sanitizer signal other than LSan.

types.name: MOVE_FROM_THEN_READ
types.description: C++ object is moved-from, then read. Moved-from objects are in a valid but unspecified state; reading non-reset fields is a logic bug and occasionally UB.

types.name: REFERENCE_TO_TEMPORARY
types.description: const T& x = f().member where f() returns a temporary that is destroyed at end of the full expression, leaving x dangling.

types.name: STRICT_ALIASING_VIOLATION
types.description: Access to an object through a pointer of an incompatible type. UB under -fstrict-aliasing; the compiler may hoist or reorder loads.

types.name: DATA_RACE
types.description: Concurrent access to the same non-atomic variable from two threads where at least one access is a write, without synchronization. UB under the C and C++ memory models.

types.name: TOCTOU
types.description: Check-then-act sequence where a separate thread, process, or filesystem actor can mutate state between the check and the act. Includes filesystem, shared-memory, and signal-handler variants.

types.name: LOCK_ORDER_INVERSION
types.description: Two code paths acquire two locks in opposite orders, creating a deadlock potential.

types.name: MISSING_ATOMIC
types.description: Shared variable read-modify-write implemented with raw x++ or x = x | mask instead of atomic. Often silent, not reliably tripped by TSan.

types.name: SIGNAL_UNSAFE_CALL
types.description: Signal handler calls functions that are not async-signal-safe (malloc, printf, mutex lock, most of libc).

types.name: THREAD_CANCEL_IN_CRITICAL
types.description: pthread_cancel or C++ thread termination arrives while a thread holds a mutex or owns a resource with no cleanup handler. Results in stranded locks or leaked resources.

types.name: FORMAT_STRING
types.description: Attacker-controlled string passed as the format argument to a variadic format function.

types.name: UNVALIDATED_LENGTH
types.description: Length field read from untrusted source (network, file, IPC) and used as the size argument to memcpy, read, a loop bound, or an allocation without being bounded against the actual buffer.

types.name: INTEGER_PARSE_NO_RANGE
types.description: String-to-integer conversion whose result is used without checking errno, range, or saturation. atoi has no error signaling at all.

types.name: NUL_TERMINATOR_ASSUMPTION
types.description: External buffer (network read, file read, IPC) is passed to strlen, strcpy, strcmp etc. without first ensuring a NUL byte exists within the known bounds.

types.name: UNTRUSTED_SIZE_IN_MEMCPY
types.description: Size argument to memcpy / memmove / read is computed from external data using arithmetic that can overflow or wrap. Pairs with A06 and A05.

types.name: PATH_TRAVERSAL
types.description: External path segment passed to open, fopen, stat, unlink etc. without canonicalization or prefix containment.

types.name: SIGNED_OVERFLOW
types.description: Signed integer overflow. UB in C and C++. Compilers at -O2 routinely eliminate overflow-check code that assumes signed wrap.

types.name: SHIFT_EXCESS
types.description: Left or right shift by an amount greater than or equal to the type's bit width, or left shift that overflows a signed type. UB.

types.name: UNSEQUENCED_SIDE_EFFECT
types.description: Multiple unsequenced modifications to the same scalar (i = i++ + ++i;) or side-effect in a function-call argument list whose order is unspecified.

types.name: UNINITIALIZED_READ
types.description: Read of an object whose value has not been set. malloc does not zero; POD members of a no-init constructor are undefined; partial struct initializers leave fields undefined.

types.name: PTR_ARITH_OOB
types.description: Pointer arithmetic that produces a pointer outside the object it was derived from (other than one-past-the-end). UB.

types.name: MODIFY_CONST
types.description: Cast-away-const followed by write; writing to string literals; modifying objects declared const.

types.name: RAII_BYPASS
types.description: Resource acquired with a raw allocator (malloc, fopen, open, socket, pthread_mutex_lock) without an RAII wrapper, and at least one code path (early return, exception, goto err) omits the release.
markers: fopen without fclose on error path
open without close on error path

types.name: FD_LEAK
types.description: File-descriptor leak on error path. Specialization of O01 with stricter severity because FD exhaustion denies all new sockets or files system-wide.

types.name: DTOR_THROWS
types.description: C++ destructor that can throw. If thrown during stack unwinding from another exception, std::terminate is called.

types.name: NOEXCEPT_VIOLATION
types.description: Function declared noexcept that calls a function which can throw. Any escaped exception calls std::terminate.

types.name: MUTEX_NOT_UNLOCKED
types.description: Raw pthread_mutex_lock or mtx.lock() with a path (error, exception) that returns without unlocking. Subsequent acquires deadlock.

types.name: SECRET_CLEAR_DSE
types.description: memset(secret, 0, n) at end of function that the compiler eliminates as dead-store because secret is not subsequently read. Key material remains on the stack.
